LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT GORDON UHLMANN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION DENIED AS TO BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION AS A MATTER OF LAW BASED ON THE MERGER DOCTRINE. MOTION GRANTED AS TO DECLARATORY RELIEF AND INJUNCTION IN UHLMANN'S FAVOR. INJUNCTION TO OPERATE PROSPECTIVELY, I.E. HIN LAND DOES NOT NEED TO REPAINT THE HOUSE. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE TERMS OF THE INJUNCTION. ALSO, THE COURT OVERRULES ALL OBJECTIONS. ((MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS COMPLAINANT GORDON UHLMANN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: James S. Sifers, Esq. of Madison Harbor ALC (949) 756-9050 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiffs/Cross-defendant Interfusion Technology LLC and opposing party; Joseph S. May, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 781-3333 appearing for Defendant/cross-complainant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to breach of contract cause of action as a matter of law based on the merger doctrine. Motion granted as to declaratory relief and injunction in Uhlmann's favor. Injunction to operate prospectively, i.e. Hin Land does not need to repaint the house. Parties to meet and confer regarding the terms of the injunction. Also, the Court overrules all objections.((Moving party to prepare a form of order.) 302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
